[{"motor_ref":"45189fc5","motor_type":"MathjaxBlank","motor_json":"{\"problemText\":\"Two trumpet players, Clora and Dizzy, try to get their instruments in-tune by playing the same note, B$\\\\flat\\\\,.$ \\n\\nAt first, they are playing very different-sounding pitches, so they make some adjustments to their instruments until they're playing the same pitch, which they think is B$\\\\flat$ at $233.1\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}\\\\,.$ \\n\\nHowever, the band leader comes along with a third, properly tuned trumpet. They find that both Clora and Dizzy's trumpets play a pitch too low, and each produces $f_{\\\\text{beat}}=3.5\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}$ when played with the true B$\\\\flat$ at $233.1\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}\\\\,.$\\n\\n[b]What frequency are Clora and Dizzy playing together on their trumpets, after their adjustments? Round your answer to the nearest hertz.[\\/b]\\n\",\"barChartText\":null,\"type\":\"MathjaxBlank\",\"parseFormat\":\"values\",\"outputDisplayFormat\":\"inlineNewJax\",\"barChartFormat\":null,\"options\":{\"mathjax\":\"\\\\[\\\\blank{??????}\\\\;\\\\mathrm{Hz}\\\\;.\\\\]\",\"allAllowedCharacters\":\"102345789\",\"maxLength\":[4]},\"problem_text_fmt\":\"Two trumpet players, Clora and Dizzy, try to get their instruments in-tune by playing the same note, B<\\/span>
\\n
\\nAt first, they are playing very different-sounding pitches, so they make some adjustments to their instruments until they're playing the same pitch, which they think is B<\\/span> at
\\n
\\nHowever, the band leader comes along with a third, properly tuned trumpet. They find that both Clora and Dizzy's trumpets play a pitch too low, and each produces when played with the true B<\\/span> at
\\n
\\nWhat frequency are Clora and Dizzy playing together on their trumpets, after their adjustments? Round your answer to the nearest hertz.<\\/b>
\\n\"}"}]
solution_text:
Clora and Dizzy are playing the frequency $ oxed{230\;\mathrm{Hz}}\,.$

We know their frequency is too low, the beat frequency is $f_{	ext{beat}}=3.5\;\mathrm{Hz}\,,$ and the properly tuned trumpet plays $233.1\;\mathrm{Hz}\,.$

So Clora and Dizzy's frequency $f$ is given by \[ 233.1\;\mathrm{Hz} - f = 3.5\;\mathrm{Hz}\,.\]

That gives a frequency of \[ 233.1\;\mathrm{Hz} -  3.5\;\mathrm{Hz} = 229.6\;\mathrm{Hz}\;\]
